name: Government of India Act 1919 (Montagu–Chelmsford Reforms)
era: Crown Rule — Constitutional Frameworks of British India (1858–1947)
key_provisions: Dyarchy in provinces: 'transferred' subjects (education, health, local government) under Indian ministers; 'reserved' subjects (police, revenue, justice) under the Governor
Bicameral central legislature: Council of State and Legislative Assembly
Separate electorates extended to Sikhs and others
Statutory review after ten years — producing the Simon Commission and the Round Table Conferences
demise: Replaced by the Government of India Act 1935 after the dyarchy experiment was judged unworkable.
